era: Indie Pop. E=4 -- power pop precision, hooks first, Merge Records enters. v1.49.351.

location.formative: Capitol Hill indie scene. Power pop lineage: Big Star, Cheap Trick, Matthew Sweet.
location.career: Telekinesis. 2009-present. Merge Records. Michael Benjamin Lerner solo project.

members.name: Michael Benjamin Lerner
members.note: Songwriter, multi-instrumentalist, drummer-first. The sole creative core.

theory_nodes.concept: Merge Records enters the 360 at degree 210 -- first appearance. Durham, NC label. Founded 1989 by McCaughan/Ballance. Arcade Fire, Neutral Milk Hotel, Superchunk. National indie scouting Seattle.
examples: Roster: Arcade Fire, Neutral Milk Hotel, Spoon, Caribou.
Telekinesis: Merge reaches across the country for a Seattle songwriter.
theory_nodes.description: Merge Records enters. National indie label. Durham NC to Capitol Hill Seattle.

genealogy.relationship: Telekinesis at 210. First Merge entry in the 360.

theory_nodes.concept: Power pop paradox -- maximum emotional impact at moderate volume. The 'power' is in the songwriting, not the amplifier. E=4 captures powerful hooks with restrained delivery.
examples: Big Star: hooks that outlived the band.
Cheap Trick: arena hooks at club volume.
Telekinesis: one person, maximum melody, E=4.
The power is in the precision.
theory_nodes.description: Power pop paradox. Maximum melody at moderate energy. Precision over volume.

theory_nodes.concept: Solo project as full band -- Lerner writes, records, performs all instruments. Studio multi-tracking creates the illusion of a band. The craft of making solo sound communal.
examples: Telekinesis: one person, full band sound.
Moving things with the mind -- name as metaphor.
Merge signing a solo project = confidence in songwriter.
theory_nodes.description: Solo project as full band. Multi-tracking illusion. The songwriter as the whole.

genealogy.relationship: Telekinesis at 210. Solo project on Merge Records.

cross_references.relationship: Previous degree. Self-released (209) to Merge Records (210). DIY to major indie label.
Same E=4. Capitol Hill. Slack/ironic (201) vs. tight/earnest (210). Same energy, opposite postures.
Next degree. World/indie fusion. Back to self-released after Merge.
Hardly Art streak end. Network/supergroup (204) vs. one-man project (210).
